summary: Group favourites after an eye-catching Euro 2024 under Murat Yakin — fluid in possession, switching between a 3-4-3 and 4-3-3, with elite defensive structure (2nd-best defence, 2nd-lowest xG against). An experienced squad orchestrated by captain Granit Xhaka.
style: Dynamic — moves between 3-4-3 and 4-3-3, with Aebischer roaming into midfield from wing-back, Vargas and Ndoye drifting inside from the wings, and full-backs pushing on (Widmer often higher than Rodríguez). Qualifiers were a more settled 4-2-3-1. Build through Xhaka, Rodríguez, Akanji and Elvedi, finding runners behind and switching play.

counterReliance.note: Fluid possession side backed by elite structure; breaks with a quick, interchanging front line

strengths: Fluid front line (Vargas, Ndoye, Embolo + Rieder) with licence to interchange
Deep experience — 8 of the likely XI have 50+ caps
Xhaka's line-breaking passing as the progression engine
weaknesses: Formation still fluid between systems — cohesion depends on the personnel chosen

keyPlayers.name: Granit Xhaka
keyPlayers.note: Closing on 150 caps; intercepts and wins duels off the ball, and his line-breaking passes are over Switzerland's qualifying goals

keyPlayers.name: Silvan Widmer
keyPlayers.note: A threat inside the box with his in-field movement — scored from advanced runs vs Kosovo

keyPlayers.name: Dan Ndoye
keyPlayers.note: Runs behind the line; the finish to Switzerland's slick interchange moves

tactics: Build patiently through the experienced spine, switch play, and release an interchanging front three behind the defence; defend with structure and break at pace. Hard to play through, dangerous on the move.
notes: The flexible build-up could be tested by Canada's high press in a potentially decisive final group game.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group B (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
